summary: A Go monorepo project currently in an aggressive test coverage optimization phase, heavily utilizing AI agents to generate supplementary tests.
concerns: Over-reliance on AI-generated test files (glm-agents) may introduce brittle or superficial tests
Test files named 'coverage_boost' suggest testing for metrics rather than behavior
High volume of incremental test agent directories (0005-0045) indicates fragmented test generation history
Potential test maintenance burden due to duplicated test logic across agent directories
suggestions: Consolidate and clean up the GOrchestra/glm-agents directories to remove redundant test artifacts
Refactor 'coverage_boost' tests to focus on meaningful edge cases and behavior instead of line coverage
Establish guardrails for AI-generated tests to ensure they test business logic, not just increase percentages
Review core source files (non-test) to understand actual domain logic, as only test files are highlighted
